_meta.policy: single source of truth for every Section-7 number cited in the paper, e-companion, or site

claimA.provenance: General gpt-4o census. Reuses amazon_category_pair_lift.parquet (also_buy + also_view); only census + re-derived anchors are new.
claimA.dataset: Amazon Product Co-purchase Graph (also_buy / also_view lift between leaf categories)
claimA.anchors: substitute=taxonomic (CA 6-digit prefix / US trilateral_code, both mapped); random=within mapped slice, seed 42

claimA.provenance: General gpt-4o census. Reuses amazon_category_pair_lift.parquet (also_buy + also_view); only census + re-derived anchors are new.
claimA.dataset: Amazon Product Co-purchase Graph (also_buy / also_view lift between leaf categories)
claimA.anchors: substitute=taxonomic (CA 6-digit prefix / US trilateral_code, both mapped); random=within mapped slice, seed 42

yelp_CA_main.provenance: General gpt-4o census. PARTIAL recovered Yelp co-visitation aggregate (recoverable-lift coverage reported honestly).
yelp_CA_main.dataset: Yelp Open Dataset (user co-visitation lift between business categories; partial recovery)
yelp_CA_main.anchors: substitute=taxonomic (CA 6-digit prefix / US trilateral_code, both mapped); random=within mapped slice, seed 42

yelp_US_main.provenance: General gpt-4o census. PARTIAL recovered Yelp co-visitation aggregate (recoverable-lift coverage reported honestly).
yelp_US_main.dataset: Yelp Open Dataset (user co-visitation lift between business categories; partial recovery)
yelp_US_main.anchors: substitute=taxonomic (CA 6-digit prefix / US trilateral_code, both mapped); random=within mapped slice, seed 42

feasibility.metros: Edmonton CMA only (Edmonton + Sherwood Park, St. Albert, Beaumont, Spruce Grove)
feasibility.caveat: Single metro (Edmonton, AB). Above the ~2000-business / category bar, so feasible, but geographically narrow.

covisitation_stream.coverage_note: ~84% of the ~6.99M-review file streamed in one decompress window; isal stream non-resumable across sandbox calls and full stream (~55s) exceeds the ~45s ceiling, so last ~16% not reached. Edmonton reviews are date-interleaved (not geographically clustered), so the kept set is a representative sample.

provenance.source: StatCan 36-10-0001 (Input-Output tables, detail level), Symmetric I-O.
provenance.construct: Cross-industry CO-USE + sector supply-chain linkage; CONVERGENT supply-side evidence for PRODUCTION complementarity.
provenance.census: general-rubric census, primary gpt-4o, Canada (7-digit NAPCS).
provenance.vs_bea: Same-economy (Canada) + recent (2023) vs 1997 US BEA fallback (different economy, 26y older).

linkage_measures.couse: cosine of sector use-rows across intermediate using-industry cols (log1p,L2)
linkage_measures.supplychain: symmetric max of column-normalized direct-requirement coefficients (sector x sector)

crosswalk.source: NAPCS L4 title+definition -> StatCan sector title (rapidfuzz token_set_ratio); NON-CIRCULAR.

primary.note: Primary = confidence>=70 guarded crosswalk (content-token guard), co-use measure. The content guard removes stopword-only false matches; with it the complement>random signal is monotone and present at ALL thresholds (AUC ~0.57-0.67), rising as concordance tightens. THR70 balances N (~1000 overall / ~740 production) and concordance quality; THR60 (large N) and THR80 (cleanest, smaller N) retained in the sweep.

provenance.source: BEA 2017 benchmark DETAIL I-O accounts, after redefinitions, producers' prices (Use table).
provenance.construct: PRODUCTION/supply-chain linkage + cross-industry co-use; CONVERGENT (supply-side) evidence for the PRODUCTION complement construct, NOT consumer co-use.
provenance.census: general-rubric census, generator gpt-4o, both economies (CA, US).
provenance.production_definition: production complement subset = BOTH endpoints have gpt4o_consumer_final == False.

linkage_measures.couse: cosine of commodity use-rows across intermediate-industry columns (log1p, L2)
linkage_measures.supplychain: symmetric max of column-normalized direct-requirement coefficients

crosswalk.source: built fresh: CA NAPCS title -> BEA 2017 detail commodity title (rapidfuzz token_set_ratio)
built fresh: US NAPCS title -> BEA 2017 detail commodity title (rapidfuzz token_set_ratio)
built fresh: CA NAPCS title -> BEA 2017 detail commodity title (rapidfuzz token_set_ratio)
built fresh: US NAPCS title -> BEA 2017 detail commodity title (rapidfuzz token_set_ratio)

bea_2017_US.gemini_coverage_note: CORRECTED 2026-06-18: the gemini-2.5-pro census is now the FULL census (CA 3049 / US 3520 focals), matching the gpt-4o census. Earlier gemini numbers in this folder were a PARTIAL-CENSUS ARTIFACT (only CA 598 / US 562 focals were visible mid-write on the mount; mapped n_comp was ~264-409 CA and ~563-1367 US, yielding noisy sub-0.5 AUCs that spuriously suggested non-replication). With the full census the gemini replication now AGREES with the gpt-4o primary: Claim A AUC(complement>random) CA couse 0.574 / supplychain 0.605, US couse 0.600 / supplychain 0.603 (gpt-4o primary: CA 0.579/0.595, US 0.578/0.572), on mapped n_comp ~5346 CA / ~8673 US (vs the prior few hundred). The production-side finding replicates across both generators.

stability_check_auc_complement_vs_random.note: production-side finding is vintage-stable across the three detail benchmark years on the same Use-table sheets.
